subject: [DMaaP MR] Use HTTPS only for external traffic
commit_message:
[DMaaP MR] Use HTTPS only for external traffic

Use the new template features to create two services for DMaaP Message
Router:
 * One of ClusterIP type with HTTP AND HTTPS port, with the same
   name as before.
 * Another of NodePort type with HTTPS only port, with a new name (this
   one should be used only for external traffic)

I've also replaced `tabs` iby spaces in two resources files so it's
easier to read the configmap.

Issue-ID: DMAAP-1400
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: I394e0b5327ffe2605f0c4c8b134e49553b06232c

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 1 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange 1: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 1:

here's the (partial) output of `helm template`:

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  annotations:
    msb.onap.org/service-info: '[
        {
            "serviceName": "message-router",
            "version": "v1",
            "url": "/",
            "protocol": "REST",
            "port": "api",
            "visualRange":"1"
        }
        ]'
  name: message-router
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: message-router
    helm.sh/chart: message-router-5.0.0
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: release
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Tiller
spec:
  ports:

    - port: 3905
      targetPort: api
      name: https-api
    - port: 3904
      targetPort: api-plain
      name: http-api
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: message-router
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: release
---

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:

  name: message-router-external
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: message-router
    helm.sh/chart: message-router-5.0.0
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: release
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Tiller
spec:
  ports:

    - port: 3905
      targetPort: api
      name: https-api
      nodePort: 30226
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: message-router
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: release

---

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: release-message-router
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: message-router
    helm.sh/chart: message-router-5.0.0
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: release
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Tiller
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: message-router
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: release
  serviceName: message-router
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:

      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: message-router
        helm.sh/chart: message-router-5.0.0
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: release
        app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Tiller
      name: message-router
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - command:
        - /root/ready.py
        args:
        - --container-name
        - message-router-kafka
        - --container-name
        - message-router-zookeeper
        env:
        - name: NAMESPACE
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace
        image: "oomk8s/readiness-check:2.0.0"
        imagePullPolicy: Always
        name: message-router-readiness
      containers:
        - name: message-router
          image: "nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/dmaap/dmaap-mr:1.1.17"
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:

          - containerPort: 3905
            name: api
          - containerPort: 3904
            name: api-plain
          livenessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: api
            initialDelaySeconds: 70
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 1
          readinessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: api
            initialDelaySeconds: 70
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 1
          env:
          - name: enableCadi
            value: "true"
          volumeMounts:
          - mountPath: /etc/localtime
            name: localtime
            readOnly: true
          - mountPath: /appl/dmaapMR1/bundleconfig/etc/appprops/MsgRtrApi.properties
            subPath: MsgRtrApi.properties
            name: appprops
          - mountPath: /appl/dmaapMR1/bundleconfig/etc/logback.xml
            subPath: logback.xml
            name: logback
          - mountPath: /appl/dmaapMR1/etc/cadi.properties
            subPath: cadi.properties
            name: cadi
          - mountPath: /appl/dmaapMR1/etc/keyfile
            subPath: mykey
            name: mykey
          resources:
                        limits:
                          cpu: 2000m
                          memory: 4Gi
                        requests:
                          cpu: 500m
                          memory: 1Gi

      volumes:
        - name: localtime
          hostPath:
            path: /etc/localtime
        - name: appprops
          configMap:
            name: release-message-router-msgrtrapi-prop-configmap
        - name: logback
          configMap:
            name: release-message-router-logback-xml-configmap
        - name: cadi
          configMap:
            name: release-message-router-cadi-prop-configmap
        - name: mykey
          secret:
            secretName: release-message-router-secret
      imagePullSecrets:
      - name: "default-docker-registry-key"

Patch Set 1:

Sylvain Desbureaux, I am assuming as of today all the ONAP applications are deployed in a single cluster. So  I dont think DCAE or any application have any  need for accessing the DMaaP Message Router service from outside cluster using the NodePort. 

If our goal is to close the HTTP traffic to a service from services that reside inside and outside cluster, this change may not help. If out goal is to close the HTTP traffic originated from outside the cluster we can proceed with this change.
